description_long: This object is a vintage-style instant camera with a boxy, rectangular body and a prominent bellows mechanism extending from the front. The camera features a large circular lens at the front, flanked by a flexible accordion-style bellows, and a side-mounted grip with a cable release. The top section houses a viewfinder window and a film compartment, with various buttons and dials visible for manual operation. The rear panel includes a film door with a latch and a secondary viewfinder window, suggesting manual film loading. The exterior is primarily matte black or dark brown plastic or metal, with some metallic and rubberized components for grip and durability. The overall design is robust and utilitarian, suitable for handheld use in urban environments.
description: A vintage instant camera with a boxy body, bellows, and a large front lens, featuring manual controls and a side grip.
description_view_0: Side view showing the extended bellows, side grip, and cable release, with the lens partially visible.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the large circular lens, bellows, viewfinder, and manual controls on the top section.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the bellows, lens, and grip visible, showing the camera's depth and side details.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the film door, latch, and viewfinder window, with the grip and top controls partially visible.
